name: sonarcloud
purpose: Unified orchestrator for all SonarCloud operations against the TDMS — sync issues from API, resolve fixed items, generate reports, check quality gate, run cleanup sprints.
notes: v1.0 2026-02-05. Not portable: deep TDMS integration (MASTER_DEBT.jsonl, intake scripts, views pipeline, SonarCloud project key hardcoded). Consolidates sonarcloud-sprint and sync-sonarcloud-debt. Has MCP server at scripts/mcp/sonarcloud-server.js. Scope=user due to SONAR_TOKEN requirement.

name: test-suite
purpose: Multi-phase UI testing orchestration for SoNash using Playwright MCP or Chrome Extension. Runs smoke, feature protocol, security, and performance checks against deployment URLs.
notes: v1.0 2026-02-07. Not portable: SoNash-specific test routes, production URL, and test protocol JSON files are all product-specific. Protocol-driven testing architecture (*.protocol.json files in .claude/test-protocols/) is methodology-portable but content is product-specific. State: .claude/test-results/. Playwright MCP dependency makes this cross-platform viable if URL and protocols are replaced.

name: add-debt
purpose: Track technical debt items in the canonical TDMS tracker (MASTER_DEBT.jsonl). Supports PR-deferred and manual ad-hoc workflows with interactive preview and duplicate detection.
notes: v2.0 2026-03-20 (32-decision skill audit rewrite). JASON-OS has v0.1-stub of this skill that appends to .planning/DEBT_LOG.md instead of MASTER_DEBT.jsonl. SoNash version has full TDMS integration with SHA256 content-hash dedup, severity gating (S0 cannot be deferred), batch mode. The workflow and UX patterns (preview, confirmation, duplicate detection) are portable — only the storage backend differs. sanitize_fields = TDMS path configuration.

name: audit-code
purpose: Single-session code review audit across 7 categories (Hygiene, Types, Framework, Testing, Security, AICode, Debugging) with parallel 3-agent or sequential fallback execution.
sanitize_fields: SonarCloud baseline counts (778 issues as of 2026-01-05)
notes: v1.0 2026-02-25. Parallel mode spawns 3 independent subagents (hygiene-and-types, framework-and-testing, security-and-debugging) via Task tool. CRITICAL dependency: _shared/AUDIT_TEMPLATE.md for standard procedures. Also references Code Review Plan template and FALSE_POSITIVES database. Episodic memory MCP is a soft dep (pre-audit context). The 7-category structure and AICode-specific patterns are portable and valuable.

name: pre-commit-fixer
purpose: Diagnose and fix pre-commit hook failures through classify-fix-report-confirm workflow. Reads .git/hook-output.log, spawns targeted subagents, presents structured report before re-committing.
sanitize_fields: failure category table (oxlint, pattern compliance, doc index, skill validation, debt/schema checks are SoNash-specific)
notes: v2.0 2026-03-22 (47-decision skill audit, 41->78 lines). JASON-OS has v2.0-jasonos-v0.1 port that is foundation-scoped (gitleaks-only .husky/pre-commit scaffold). SoNash version is the full production skill. State: .claude/tmp/pre-commit-fixer-state.json. References CLAUDE.md guardrails #9/#13/#14 directly (portable). The classify-fix-report-confirm workflow pattern is portable; SoNash-specific failure categories (oxlint, pattern compliance, doc index) are sanitize targets. Subagents: debugger, code-reviewer, general-purpose types.

name: audit-performance
purpose: Single-session performance audit across 7 categories (Bundle, Rendering, Data Fetching, Memory, Core Web Vitals, Offline Support, AI Performance Patterns) with parallel 2-agent or sequential fallback.
notes: v1.0 2026-02-25. Parallel: 2 agents (bundle-and-rendering, data-and-memory). AI Performance Patterns category added 2026-02-02 — includes Firebase-specific checks (unbounded Firestore queries, onSnapshot vs one-time fetch). The performance audit structure and categories are largely portable after sanitizing SoNash-specific file paths. The episodic memory MCP calls are a soft dep.

name: audit-engineering-productivity
purpose: Single-session engineering productivity audit across 3 domains (Developer Golden Path, Debugging Ergonomics, Offline Support) with parallel 3-agent or sequential fallback.
notes: v1.1 2026-02-23. Parallel: 3 agents (dx-golden-path-auditor, debugging-ergonomics-auditor, offline-support-auditor). Firebase persistence and service worker checks are SoNash-specific. The DX/debugging/offline structure is broadly applicable to any web project. Called as Stage 2 of /audit-comprehensive. Mentions AUDIT_TRACKER.md and commit thresholds for trigger logic.

name: content-research-writer
purpose: Writing partner for research, outlining, drafting, and refining long-form content (blog posts, articles, technical tutorials, thought leadership) while preserving author voice.
notes: v1.1 2026-02-25 (trimmed to <500 lines, extracted examples to examples.md). 2-file layout: SKILL.md + examples.md. No project-specific dependencies. Fully portable. Use case is universal (any writing project). The skill is a workflow guide for collaborative writing — no scripts, no SoNash-specific paths. Minor: references VS Code as preferred editor (cosmetic, not a hard dep).

name: schemas
purpose: Zod schema definitions and CLI validator for CAS (Content Analysis System) artifacts: analysis.json (v4.2 format), findings.jsonl entries, and the validate-artifact.ts CLI entry point.
sanitize_fields: CAS-specific schema fields (last_synthesized_at, skillVersion, repoType, etc.)
notes: UNUSUAL: This is not a skill — there is no SKILL.md. Three TypeScript files only: analysis-schema.ts (Zod schema for repo-analysis analysis.json v4.2), findings-schema.ts (Zod schema for findings.jsonl), validate-artifact.ts (CLI validator entry point). Type is effectively 'tool-file' or 'script-lib'. Classified as skill because it lives in .claude/skills/ but lacks SKILL.md — possible oversight or intentional (shared code library, not a slash command). Imports from scripts/lib/ via relative path. Should be tagged type: script-lib in synthesis. No frontmatter. Cross-referenced by CAS handler skills (repo-analysis, website-analysis, etc.) as scripts/lib/analysis-schema.js (Note: the skills/ version is TypeScript; scripts/lib/ version is JavaScript — these may be different files).

name: excel-analysis
purpose: Analyze Excel spreadsheets using pandas Python: pivot tables, charts, data cleaning, merging, and formatting. Reference card for common Excel operations.
notes: v1.0 2026-02-25. Fully portable Python recipe collection. No project-specific dependencies. Frontmatter has unusual name field 'Excel Analysis' (capital E, space) — the directory name is excel-analysis. This is a reference-style skill (code snippets) rather than a workflow skill. No scripts, no agents. Universal scope.

name: find-skills
purpose: Help users discover and install skills from the skills.sh ecosystem and Claude Code plugin marketplaces via unified search script.
notes: v1.0 2026-02-25. Spawn prompt says 'may be ported' to JASON-OS — NOT present in JASON-OS. The skills.sh and plugin marketplace discovery workflow is universal. The unified search script (scripts/search-capabilities.js) is SoNash-specific. Sanitize target: replace scripts/search-capabilities.js with a portable alternative or remove that step. The fallback (npx skills find) works without the script.

name: frontend-design
purpose: Guide creation of distinctive production-grade frontend interfaces that avoid generic AI aesthetics. Emphasizes intentional design direction, typography, animation, and spatial composition.
notes: v1.0 2026-02-25. Fully portable design philosophy skill. No scripts, no project-specific deps. Has license field in frontmatter ('Complete terms in LICENSE.txt') — no LICENSE.txt found in the directory (single file). The skill is pure prose guidance for aesthetic design decisions. Universal scope — applies to any frontend project.

name: gh-fix-ci
purpose: Inspect GitHub PR checks with gh CLI, pull failing GitHub Actions logs, summarize failure context, then create a fix plan and implement after user approval.
notes: v1.0 2026-02-25. Has a bundled Python script (scripts/inspect_pr_checks.py) for fetching PR checks and logs — but the file listing shows only SKILL.md (1 file). The script path reference in SKILL.md uses '<path-to-skill>/scripts/inspect_pr_checks.py' which implies the script should be in a scripts/ subdirectory but it is absent. Either the script was moved out or never created. The skill workflow uses gh CLI directly as fallback. References 'plan skill' for drafting fix plans. Dependency on sandbox_permissions=require_escalated for gh auth. Portable once script path is resolved.

name: using-superpowers
purpose: Establish the behavior of checking for applicable skills BEFORE ANY RESPONSE, including clarifying questions. The foundational skill-first discipline enforcer.
notes: v1.0 2026-02-25. Fully portable behavioral instruction skill. JASON-OS spawn prompt says 'ported' but NOT present in JASON-OS .claude/skills/ — only in CLAUDE.md references. The EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT block is notable for its unusually emphatic mandatory language. No scripts, no project deps. The dot-graph diagram shows the skill decision flow. Universal applicability.

name: checkpoint
purpose: Save session state for recovery after compaction or failures. Writes to .claude/state/ files (local) and optionally to MCP memory graph (--mcp flag).
notes: v2.0 2026-02-14 (version history says 1.0 only — no v2.0 entry, version in frontmatter shows 2.0). JASON-OS HAS this skill ported (confirmed in spawn prompt). No SoNash-specific content. Writes to .claude/state/handoff.json and .claude/state/task-{name}.state.json. MCP memory save is optional (--mcp flag). Universal scope — any project can use this.

name: audit-health
purpose: Meta-check for the SoNash audit system health — runs 4 diagnostic scripts, checks ecosystem audit skill coverage, and recommends next audits to run.
notes: v1.1 2026-02-24. Not portable: all 4 scripts (audit-health-check.js, count-commits-since.js, validate-templates.js, pre-audit-check.js) are SoNash-specific audit infrastructure. The 9 health check dimensions and 7 ecosystem audit coverage table (hook, session, tdms, pr, skill, doc, script) are SoNash-specific. The meta-audit concept is portable as a pattern but the implementation is deeply tied to SoNash's audit script infrastructure.

name: sonash-context
purpose: SoNash project context (stack versions, architecture, security boundaries) injected into agent definitions via the 'skills:' frontmatter field. Prevents agents from flagging modern stack versions as invalid.
notes: v1.1 2026-04-15 (added required SKILL_STANDARDS.md sections). KEY SKILL: This is the mechanism behind 'skills: [sonash-context]' in agent frontmatter. When an agent declares this, the skill content is injected as context before the agent runs. Content: Next.js 16.2.0, React 19.2.4, Firebase 12.10.0, Tailwind CSS 4.2.2, Zod 4.3.6, TypeScript strict mode. Also includes architecture patterns (repository pattern, state management, Zod validation) and security boundaries (no direct Firestore writes, httpsCallable pattern, App Check, sanitizeError). Not portable by design — this is SoNash-specific project context. The JASON-OS equivalent would be a jason-os-context skill with JASON-OS stack facts. The injection mechanism itself (skills: field in agent frontmatter) IS portable.

name: quick-fix
purpose: Auto-suggest fixes for common pre-commit and pattern compliance issues. Advisory skill that parses error output, categorizes by type, and offers inline fix commands or auto-applies lint fixes.
notes: v1.0 2026-02-25. Advisory-only skill (suggests, does not require confirmation before auto-fixing). Distinct from pre-commit-fixer (which is operational with gates). Noted in pre-commit-fixer SKILL.md as 'advisory not operational'. Pattern compliance table references SoNash-specific patterns (Direct Firestore -> repository, console.log -> logger). The ESLint fix workflow is portable. Quick-fix is explicitly called out in the hook integration section as a 'proposed' hook trigger — not yet wired.

name: validate-claude-folder
purpose: Validate .claude folder configuration consistency: MCP servers, hook files, skill/command alignment, documentation freshness, secrets configuration, and agent file frontmatter.
sanitize_fields: Hook names list in bash loop (session-start, check-mcp-servers, check-write-requirements, check-edit-requirements, pattern-check, analyze-user-request — SoNash-specific)
notes: v1.0 2026-02-25. Hardcoded list of expected hook filenames in Step 2 — all are SoNash-specific hooks. The validation structure (MCP, hooks, skills/commands, docs, secrets, agents) is portable. Content of each check is SoNash-specific. The MCP config check (cat .mcp.json) is portable. Agent frontmatter validation (Step 6) is portable.

name: docs-maintain
purpose: Check documentation sync (template-instance validation) and auto-update doc artifacts (index regeneration, cross-doc dependencies, archive verification).
notes: v1.0 2026-02-25. Both modes (--check, --update) depend on SoNash npm scripts (docs:sync-check, docs:index). The concept of template-instance synchronization and documentation index maintenance is portable but the scripts are SoNash-specific. Has metadata: short-description field in frontmatter.

name: task-next
purpose: Show dependency-resolved next tasks from ROADMAP.md active sprint using topological sort (Kahn's algorithm) on [depends: X1, X2] annotations.
sanitize_fields: ROADMAP.md task format (SoNash-specific IDs like Track-E, M2.1)
notes: v1.0 2026-02-25. Depends on scripts/tasks/resolve-dependencies.js which parses ROADMAP.md with [depends: X] annotations. The dependency resolution concept is portable. The ROADMAP.md format and task ID system (B1, B3, CANON-0011, DEBT-0944) are SoNash-specific. Would need a portable roadmap format or script adaptation.

name: website-synthesis
purpose: DEPRECATED redirect stub. Was consolidated into /synthesize (T29, Session #271). Redirects users to /synthesize with migration guide.
notes: Deprecated 2026-04-09 (Session #271, T29 Wave 3). Status should be 'deprecated'. Redirect stub only — 1 session overlap before deletion. .planning/synthesis-consolidation/ contains full design (32 decisions, 5 waves, 15 steps). superseded_by: synthesize. Output path changed: .research/website-analysis/synthesis/ -> .research/analysis/synthesis/.

name: repo-synthesis
purpose: DEPRECATED redirect stub. Was consolidated into /synthesize (T29, Session #271). Redirects users to /synthesize with migration guide.
notes: Deprecated 2026-04-09 (Session #271, T29 Wave 3). Status should be 'deprecated'. Redirect stub only. Superseded by synthesize. Output path changed: .research/analysis/SYNTHESIS.md -> .research/analysis/synthesis/synthesis.md. superseded_by: synthesize.

name: decrypt-secrets
purpose: Decrypt encrypted MCP tokens (.env.local.encrypted) at the start of a remote session using AES-256-GCM. Produces .env.local for MCP server use.
notes: v1.0 2026-02-25. Scope=user (operator-only, handles secrets). AES-256-GCM encryption. .env.local is gitignored; .env.local.encrypted is committed. The encrypt/decrypt script pair is portable (generic AES-256-GCM). Token list in first-time setup would need updating per project. Single SKILL.md, no companion files.

name: _shared/AUDIT_TEMPLATE.md
purpose: Shared boilerplate for all single-session audit skills: evidence requirements, dual-pass verification, JSONL output format, context recovery, post-audit validation, MASTER_DEBT cross-reference, interactive review, TDMS intake, and honesty guardrails.
notes: v1.0 2026-02-24. Consumed by: audit-code, audit-performance, audit-engineering-productivity, and by extension any skill that references the standard audit procedures pattern. Also referenced by audit-process (D1d). Contains 13-point Script Security Pre-Check (based on PR #389 findings — includes path containment, symlink guard, atomic writes, JSONL per-line try/catch). This security checklist is highly portable. The TDMS integration section is the main sanitize target.
notes_type: shared-doc-lib consumers: audit-code, audit-performance, audit-engineering-productivity, audit-process (confirmed), audit-refactoring, audit-security (likely based on pattern)

name: _shared/SELF_AUDIT_PATTERN.md
purpose: Canonical implementation pattern for per-skill self-audit scripts. Defines CLI interface, 9 audit dimensions, required security helpers, state file contract, SKILL.md wiring protocol, and rollout procedures for adding self-audits to existing skills.
notes: v1.0 2026-04-14. Newest of the _shared/ files. Extracted from scripts/cas/self-audit.js (814 lines). Referenced by skill-creator (future integration planned), skill-audit (Category 12 scoring). Defines 9 dimensions: Completeness, Orphan detection, Build integrity, Gap analysis, Functional verification, Cross-reference (deterministic), Regression, Contract, Partial recovery. Dimension 6 changed from multi-agent to deterministic check (Session #281 decision). Highly portable pattern document — the state file contract and dimension framework are universal. Scripts/lib/ dependencies are the sanitize targets.
notes_type: shared-doc-lib consumers: skill-creator (future), skill-audit (Category 12), all Standard+ skills with self-audit phases

name: _shared/SKILL_STANDARDS.md
purpose: Canonical structural and quality standards for all skills: required sections (frontmatter, When to Use/NOT, Version History), attention management, skill tiers, self-audit requirements, and lifecycle management.
notes: v3.0 2026-04-04. The highest-version shared doc. Consumed by: skill-creator, skill-ecosystem-audit, and all skill maintenance workflows. Defines skill tiers (Simple/Standard/Complex), 9-dimension self-audit framework (pointer to SELF_AUDIT_PATTERN.md), MUST/SHOULD/MAY instruction hierarchy, attention management (primacy bias), and skill lifecycle management. Highly portable — the quality standards framework and instruction hierarchy apply to any skills-based system. Largely the same as JASON-OS would need. Main sanitize targets: SoNash-specific examples and scripts/debt/ references.
notes_type: shared-doc-lib consumers: skill-creator, skill-ecosystem-audit, all skill maintenance workflows

name: _shared/TAG_SUGGESTION.md
purpose: Canonical tag suggestion protocol for all CAS handler skills (repo-analysis, website-analysis, document-analysis, media-analysis). Defines when/what/how of tagging analysis.json and extraction-journal.jsonl entries.
notes: v1.0 2026-04-15. Not portable: deeply tied to CAS infrastructure (.research/tag-vocabulary.json, analysis.json.tags field, extraction-journal.jsonl rows, CONVENTIONS.md §14 vocabulary). Consumers: repo-analysis, website-analysis, document-analysis, media-analysis Phase 6c. References CONVENTIONS.md §14 (forbidden tags, naming rules, vocabulary-first growth protocol). Part of the CAS cluster that would need to port together.
notes_type: shared-doc-lib consumers: repo-analysis, website-analysis, document-analysis, media-analysis (Phase 6c Tag Suggestion)

name: _shared/ecosystem-audit/README.md
purpose: Index of the 5 shared ecosystem audit modules with usage instructions for referencing from individual ecosystem audit SKILL.md files.
notes: v1.0 2026-03-25. Documents the 5-module shared library extracted from 8 ecosystem audit skills. Consumers: all 8 ecosystem audit skills (hook, tdms, pr, session, script, doc, skill, health). States that each audit SKILL.md retains unique sections (frontmatter, when to use, routing guide, Phase 1 script, Phase 2 dashboard, category reference, data sources). The library extraction pattern itself is portable methodology.

name: _shared/ecosystem-audit/CRITICAL_RULES.md
purpose: 8 critical rules shared across all ecosystem audit skills: progress check first, run script first, always show dashboard, conversational Q&A, save after every decision, patch suggestions, TDMS entries, save decisions.
sanitize_fields: Rule 7: TDMS entries via /add-debt (portable concept, not portable path)
notes: v1.0 2026-03-25. The 8 rules are largely portable behavioral constraints. Rule 7 (TDMS entries via /add-debt) references TDMS infrastructure but the rule itself (defer findings to a debt tracker) is portable. Rule 4 explicitly bans AskUserQuestion — uses conversational Q&A instead (portable). Rule 5 (save after every decision) references .claude/tmp/{audit-name}-audit-progress.json path (portable pattern).
notes_type: shared-doc-lib consumers: doc-ecosystem-audit, skill-ecosystem-audit, health-ecosystem-audit, pr-ecosystem-audit, script-ecosystem-audit, hook-ecosystem-audit (partial — inline), tdms-ecosystem-audit, session-ecosystem-audit

name: _shared/ecosystem-audit/COMPACTION_GUARD.md
purpose: State file schema, resume/save/cleanup protocol for ecosystem audit compaction resilience. Defines progress file format (.claude/tmp/{audit-name}-audit-progress.json) and resume logic.
notes: v1.0 2026-03-25. Defines the progress JSON schema used by all 8 ecosystem audits. The compaction guard pattern (.claude/tmp/{name}-audit-progress.json with <2 hour resume window) is portable and valuable for any long-running interactive skill.

name: _shared/ecosystem-audit/FINDING_WALKTHROUGH.md
purpose: Finding card format, decision options, patch generation, delegation to /add-debt, and batch presentation rules for the interactive walkthrough phase of ecosystem audits.
notes: v1.0 2026-03-25. Defines the interactive finding card format (severity, confidence, patch suggestion, decision options). The finding card and walkthrough pattern are portable. Delegation to /add-debt for deferrals is a portable pattern (debt tool name may differ in target project).

name: _shared/ecosystem-audit/SUMMARY_AND_TRENDS.md
purpose: Summary template, trend report format, and verification re-run protocol for the post-walkthrough summary phase of ecosystem audits.
notes: v1.0 2026-03-25. Defines the end-of-audit summary format and trend comparison against prior runs (from .claude/state/{audit-name}-history.jsonl). Trend report concept is portable.

name: _shared/ecosystem-audit/CLOSURE_AND_GUARDRAILS.md
purpose: Learnings capture, invocation tracking call, guard rails for scope/compaction/consistency, and benchmark targets for ecosystem audit completion phase.
notes: v1.0 2026-03-25. Contains invocation tracking call (npx tsx scripts/reviews/write-invocation.ts) — this is the SoNash-specific invocation registry. Drop for port. Guard rails and learnings capture protocol are portable. Benchmarks define expected completion targets (A-grade thresholds).

name: shared/CONVENTIONS.md
purpose: Shared conventions for the CAS (Content Analysis System) skill family: analyze, repo-analysis, website-analysis, document-analysis, media-analysis, synthesize. Covers phase transition markers, write-to-disk-first, scoring bands, tag vocabulary, extraction journal format, and synthesis output standards.
notes: v1.1 2026-04-12. Not portable: deeply tied to CAS infrastructure (tag-vocabulary.json, content-analysis.db, extraction-journal.jsonl, analysis.json schema). Consumers confirmed from D1c/D1d: repo-analysis, website-analysis (both use ../_shared/TAG_SUGGESTION.md AND shared/CONVENTIONS.md), analyze, recall. Documents §14 tag vocabulary (8 categories: domain, technology, concept, technique, pattern, applicability, quality, taxonomic), scoring bands (Critical/Needs Work/Healthy/Excellent 0-100), phase transition marker format (=====), write-to-disk-first rule, and conversational prose rule for creator views. The 4-band scoring scale and phase marker format are portable patterns.
notes_type: shared-doc-lib consumers: analyze, recall, repo-analysis, website-analysis, document-analysis, media-analysis, synthesize (entire CAS cluster)
